requirements.description: Per-fork mode (default) — isolated container (keel-devbox) + isolated named volume (keel-devbox_keel_home_dev); two forks cannot share state unless KEEL_DEVBOX_CONTAINER_NAME override is set (Story 2.1 per-fork path preserved).
requirements.notes: Substrate-verification present via iter-257 dev-story Debug Log resolver functional smokes (KEEL_DEVBOX_SHARED unset/false → KEEL_DEVBOX_COMPOSE_PROJECT=keel-devbox, KEEL_DEVBOX_CONTAINER_NAME_RESOLVED=keel-devbox). Not wired as automated regression — no test runner at 1.0 substrate stage.

requirements.description: Shared mode — single container (keel-devbox-shared) + single named volume (keel-devbox-shared_keel_home_dev) across forks; bind source flips from fork root to fork root's PARENT directory (matches upstream cc-devbox /Users/tthew/Development pattern; architecture.md:547); fork B's pnpm devbox:start becomes a no-op health-poll.
requirements.notes: Substrate-verification present via iter-257 dev-story Debug Log resolver functional smokes (KEEL_DEVBOX_SHARED=true → KEEL_DEVBOX_COMPOSE_PROJECT=keel-devbox-shared, KEEL_DEVBOX_REPO_NAME=basename(parent), KEEL_DEVBOX_WORKSPACE=parent) + docker-compose.yml name: parameterisation smoke (compose-project prefix flips correctly). Live two-forks-attach smoke operator-workstation-deferred per backend-A.

requirements.description: Mid-use flip — pnpm devbox:env:check warns about orphaned cross-mode containers via docker inspect probe; emits stderr warning naming the orphaned container + pointing at pnpm devbox:clean; exit code unaltered (informational).
requirements.notes: Substrate-verification present via iter-257 dev-story Debug Log: Task 4 env-check orphan-container probe uses Story 2.10 PATCH-1 rc-capture pattern (rc=0; docker inspect … || rc=$?; case … ;) to survive set -e. Warning string three-site lockstep SC-8: env-check.sh emit + docs/invariants/devbox-mode.md § Mid-use flip warning + packages/devbox/README.md § Per-fork vs shared mode § Mid-use flip. Live docker inspect probe smoke operator-workstation-deferred.

requirements.description: Concurrent-Ralph behaviour in shared mode — single-operator-at-a-time BY CONVENTION (NOT by Docker enforcement); concurrent docker attach against the same container produces interleaved stdin/stdout I/O corruption (NOT auto-detach-the-first); non-Ralph operations (pnpm devbox:shell/claude/gh:auth) use docker exec + are parallel-safe across forks; operators needing true-parallel Ralph MUST revert to per-fork mode.
requirements.notes: Doc-only concurrency decision pinned in AGENTS.md § Per-fork vs shared devbox mode (Story 2.11) + docs/invariants/devbox-mode.md § Concurrency decision + Story 2.11 § Dev Notes § Concurrency decision (Option C chosen; Options A/B rejected with rationale). No mechanical runtime path exists — Docker does NOT expose second-attach-auto-detach semantic; shared-mode concurrency story is convention-first, not machinery-first. Agent guardrail pinned in AGENTS.md: MUST NOT implement second-attach-auto-detaches-first feature. ATDD for AC 4 is out-of-scope (no harness exists for cross-fork simulation; story file § Testing Standards pre-declares trace-partial-waive candidate for AC 4).

Per-AC live fresh-fork first-run verification unlocks at M4-Pro operator workstation backend-A: AC 1 per-fork mode isolation requires two forks under different KEEL_DEVBOX_CONTAINER_NAME overrides invoked sequentially to verify Compose collision on bare name + isolation under override; AC 2 shared-mode single-container requires two fork-root siblings under a shared parent dir, both with KEEL_DEVBOX_SHARED=true, sequential pnpm devbox:start from fork A then fork B to verify fork B's invocation becomes a no-op health-poll (docker inspect detects existing container) + both resolver paths land at /workspace/<parent>/{fork-a,fork-b}/; AC 3 env-check orphan probe requires flipping .envrc between per-fork and shared mid-use on a host with either keel-devbox OR keel-devbox-shared container running from the prior mode, then pnpm devbox:env:check to verify stderr warning + pointer at pnpm devbox:clean + exit code unchanged; AC 4 concurrency decision is doc-only — no mechanical runtime verification possible; spec-declared-CR-substitution applies. No Story 2.11 code change required at any live-flow verification point — resolver + compose + env-check are stateless branches on env vars.
